scope: Supported and intentionally classified tool entry points for AI agents and operators working the NCUC historical pipeline.

status_definitions.supported: Default operator-facing entry point that should be preferred for routine work.
status_definitions.compatibility_alias: Retained for older workflows; valid, but not the preferred name.
status_definitions.narrow_helper: Useful for bounded inspection or repair tasks, but not a default starting point.
status_definitions.legacy: Retained for backward compatibility or repair only; do not choose this first.

category_definitions.inspect: State inspection commands that do not usually prescribe a repair.
category_definitions.diagnose: Focused audits that explain why a workflow is blocked or degraded.
category_definitions.repair: Commands that enqueue, mutate, or repair workflow state.
category_definitions.extract: Commands that import, mine, or extract structured tariff data.
category_definitions.legacy: Superseded or older paths kept for compatibility.

tools.goal: Compact session-start summary of review, reprocess, stale, OCR, lineage, and provisional-family state.
use_when: Starting a session
Inheriting work from another agent
Choosing which sanctioned workflow to run next
avoid_when: You already know the exact queue or audit surface you need
prerequisites: Use the active SQLite database at data/db/duke_rates.db unless told otherwise

tools.goal: Summarize the current parse-review backlog using latest-attempt semantics.
use_when: Reviewing weak parses after extraction or reprocessing
Confirming whether review debt is the active bottleneck
avoid_when: You need row-level backlog details from the legacy review queue path

tools.goal: Inspect the current targeted reprocess queue.
use_when: Checking what is already queued before adding more work
Verifying queue depth before or after processing
avoid_when: You need ranked reasons for what should be queued next

tools.goal: Rank NC documents that should be reprocessed based on queue, parse, stage, OCR, and linkage signals.
use_when: Choosing the highest-value reprocess targets
Understanding why the reprocess backlog exists
avoid_when: You only need the raw queue contents

tools.goal: Show historical docs with stale or missing page/span stage artifacts.
use_when: Investigating stage freshness issues
Deciding whether stale-stage reprocessing is needed
avoid_when: You need a general orientation summary first

tools.goal: Inspect OCR queue depth and status for scanned historical PDFs.
use_when: Checking whether OCR is blocking extraction progress
Reviewing OCR queue depth before processing
avoid_when: You already know which documents need OCR and just need to enqueue them

tools.goal: List provisional tariff families that still need review or promotion.
use_when: Checking whether intake created new provisional families
Preparing provisional-family review work
avoid_when: You need lineage or provenance gaps across all historical docs

tools.goal: Primary historical intake command: mine page/span evidence, assign family keys, and create candidate historical rows.
use_when: Downloaded NCUC records need to enter the pipeline
Recovered discovery rows need to be rematerialized into historical state
avoid_when: You are working only current Duke website documents
No new or updated downloaded records exist
prerequisites: Historical PDFs must already be downloaded and registered
Do not stack this with mine-ncuc-pipeline in the same workflow

tools.goal: Compatibility alias for the page-aware intake path used by older notes or scripts.
use_when: Following an older workflow that still refers to the pre-rename command
avoid_when: You are choosing a command name for new work
You already ran ncuc-import-pipeline
prerequisites: Same intake prerequisites as ncuc-import-pipeline

tools.goal: Create minimal tariff_version rows for historical docs that have dates and paths but still lack version linkage.
use_when: Intake created historical docs without tariff_version linkage
Extraction readiness is blocked by missing version links
avoid_when: Lineage gaps are broader than simple version bootstrapping
prerequisites: Historical docs should already exist from the intake pipeline

tools.goal: Search NCUC for likely clean historical tariff/rider documents tied to known family gaps.
use_when: A family has lineage continuity but is still missing the clean historical document
A gap audit points to document acquisition rather than parser weakness
avoid_when: The exact historical PDF is already known and can be registered directly
The current bottleneck is stale extraction or parser-profile debt
prerequisites: Prefer show-lineage-gaps-nc or a missing-doc audit first when the target family is unclear

tools.goal: Run the resumable missing-document loop from search through fetch/import/bootstrap/reprocess.
use_when: You want the sanctioned end-to-end clean-document recovery loop
A missing-document target should be resumed from an intermediate stage instead of rebuilt manually
avoid_when: You have not scoped the family or target and only need a broad orientation summary
prerequisites: Use targeted family, record, or historical-document filters whenever possible
Prefer this workflow over handwritten multi-command recovery loops

tools.goal: Explain workflow state and provenance for one missing-document family or target.
use_when: You need to inspect why one target stalled
You want family-level missing-document provenance before manual intervention
avoid_when: You need a bulk grouped view of deferred reasons across many targets

tools.goal: Show persisted next-action and blocker guidance for resumable missing-document work, including a ranked actionable queue and exact next-command suggestions.
use_when: A future agent should resume missing-document recovery from persisted guidance instead of recomputing triage
You need a queue-like worklist grouped by next action and blocker
A weaker agent should start from the top actionable targets instead of scanning every persisted row
avoid_when: You need full provenance detail for exactly one target
prerequisites: A prior validate pass from workflow run-nc-missing-doc should have persisted triage metadata

tools.goal: Execute the top ranked actionable missing-document triage target through its sanctioned underlying workflow.
use_when: A weaker agent should let the tool take the next sanctioned step automatically
The ranked triage queue already exists and you want bounded forward progress without manual command selection
avoid_when: You need to inspect the ranked queue before mutating anything
No persisted triage metadata exists yet
prerequisites: A prior validate pass from workflow run-nc-missing-doc should have persisted triage metadata
Prefer workflow report-nc-missing-doc-triage first when human review of the queue is still needed

tools.goal: Execute multiple ranked actionable missing-document triage targets with bounded stop conditions.
use_when: You want bounded forward progress across several top actionable targets
A weaker agent should not have to manually repeat top-target execution one item at a time
avoid_when: You need to inspect each ranked target before any mutation
The queue is not yet persisted from a validate pass
prerequisites: A prior validate pass from workflow run-nc-missing-doc should have persisted triage metadata
Choose a conservative --max-actions value so the run stays bounded and reviewable

tools.goal: Group deferred missing-document targets by blocking reason so remediation work stays queue-driven.
use_when: You need to understand why missing-document targets are stalled
You are choosing whether remediation or manual registration is the next step
avoid_when: You already know the exact single target you need to inspect

tools.goal: Run supported missing-document remediations and immediately promote targets that become eligible.
use_when: Deferred missing-document targets are blocked on supported repairable reasons
You want the command-first remediation path instead of ad hoc SQL or notes
avoid_when: You have not reviewed deferred reasons yet
prerequisites: Inspect workflow report-nc-missing-doc-deferred first when the blocked reasons are not already known

tools.goal: Run parser profiles against linked NC historical docs and persist tariff charges.
use_when: Historical documents and tariff versions are linked and ready for extraction
Profile changes need a targeted or broad rerun
avoid_when: Version linkage is still missing
You only need to inspect diagnostic state
prerequisites: Historical docs should have effective_start and tariff_version linkage
Prefer targeted reprocessing over broad reruns when possible

tools.goal: Validate extraction results and surface anomalies after parser runs.
use_when: Checking whether extraction materially succeeded
Reviewing profile changes after a rerun
avoid_when: You only need a quick queue overview
prerequisites: Run after extract-rates-nc or a targeted reprocess pass

tools.goal: Summarize NC family-link, version-link, effective-date, and no-charge lineage gaps.
use_when: Auditing family linkage quality
Investigating why historical docs are blocked before extraction
avoid_when: You only need provenance-field coverage

tools.goal: Separate lineage blockers from warnings for each NC historical document.
use_when: You need document-level extraction readiness checks
You want blocker vs warning distinction instead of aggregate counts
avoid_when: You only need a high-level lineage summary

tools.goal: Suggest likely family assignments for stranded NC discovery rows; optional --apply persists recoverable matches.
use_when: Stranded discovery rows have enough clues for likely family matches
You want a bounded lineage repair before rerunning intake
avoid_when: You have not reviewed the suggestions or are not prepared to mutate state
prerequisites: Inspect show-lineage-gaps-nc or validate-lineage-nc first
Use --apply only after reviewing suggested matches

tools.goal: Audit missing version provenance fields and weak discovery linkage across NC historical rows.
use_when: Auditing provenance debt
Understanding missing path/hash/docket linkage
avoid_when: You only need family-link gaps

tools.goal: Show hash-backed vs path-only coverage, fingerprint presence, and artifact reuse coverage for NC historical docs.
use_when: Assessing historical document fingerprint coverage
Evaluating how much provenance can be recovered from hash-backed records
avoid_when: You only need queue or extraction state

tools.goal: Add targeted historical documents to the reprocess queue using workflow-aware filters.
use_when: The review backlog or extraction validation points to specific rerun candidates
You need to queue bounded cleanup work instead of broad reruns
avoid_when: You have not inspected the current queue or priority signals
prerequisites: Prefer reprocess show-priority-nc first when the next action is unclear

tools.goal: Queue reprocessing specifically for stale or missing page/span stages.
use_when: Stage artifacts are stale or absent and blocking extraction quality
avoid_when: Reprocess priority is driven by profile or review issues instead
prerequisites: Confirm stale-stage debt with reprocess show-stale-historical-nc

tools.goal: Estimate which documents would be affected by a specific parser profile change.
use_when: Assessing blast radius before profile-driven reprocessing
Scoping parser work before enqueueing reruns
avoid_when: You are not working on parser profiles

tools.goal: Queue reprocessing for documents affected by a specific parser profile.
use_when: A profile change needs targeted reruns
avoid_when: You have not reviewed profile impact first
prerequisites: Prefer reprocess show-profile-impact-nc before mutating the queue

tools.goal: Process queued historical rework, including stage refresh and extraction reruns.
use_when: The queue is populated and ready to run
A targeted repair pass should now be executed
avoid_when: The queue is empty
The task is only to inspect queue contents
prerequisites: Confirm queue contents with reprocess show-queue-nc when the next action is uncertain

tools.goal: Add scanned or unreadable historical documents to the OCR queue.
use_when: Scanned PDFs need OCR before extraction can proceed
avoid_when: The OCR queue is already saturated and you have not reviewed it
prerequisites: Confirm OCR need with show-ocr-queue-nc or extraction diagnostics

tools.goal: Process queued OCR work for scanned historical PDFs.
use_when: OCR jobs are queued and the environment is ready to run them
avoid_when: The queue is empty
The task is only to inspect queue status
prerequisites: Review queue state with show-ocr-queue-nc if unclear

tools.goal: Promote a provisional family into a curated family record when the auto-linking result is correct.
use_when: A provisional family has been reviewed and should become curated
avoid_when: The family remains ambiguous or looks like garbage auto-key output
prerequisites: Inspect with list-provisional-families first

tools.goal: Rank charged provisional NC families for cleanup or promotion using sanctioned review heuristics.
use_when: Provisional families still have charges so retire-provisional-garbage-nc cannot help
An agent needs inferred promotion fields instead of guessing title or schedule overrides
avoid_when: You need to mutate state directly
The problem is zero-charge provisional sprawl better handled by retire-provisional-garbage-nc
prerequisites: Inspect with list-provisional-families first when you need the full backlog

tools.goal: Retire a bad historical document row while preserving reviewable audit intent.
use_when: A specific historical document is known to be garbage or duplicate and should be removed from the active pipeline
avoid_when: You are looking for a general provisional-family cleanup command
You have not confirmed the row should be retired
prerequisites: Use only after bounded investigation

tools.goal: Narrow helper for bounded historical family mismatch inspection when the CLI does not yet expose a needed view.
use_when: Investigating a specific mismatch case beyond the standard lineage summaries
avoid_when: A supported CLI command already answers the question
You are choosing a default workflow for a new agent

tools.goal: Older current-document review queue surface retained for compatibility.
use_when: Maintaining an older current-document workflow that still depends on it
avoid_when: Working the NC historical pipeline
Choosing a review surface for new workflows

tools.goal: Older row-level parse review queue surface retained for compatibility and detailed inspection.
use_when: A legacy workflow explicitly asks for it
avoid_when: Choosing a review surface for a new session
You only need backlog summary and prioritization

tools.goal: Older JSON-oriented NCUC ingest flow retained for compatibility and repair work.
use_when: Repairing or investigating a legacy JSON export/import path
avoid_when: Running the current historical NCUC pipeline
Choosing an intake path for new work
